items.whisper: Mrs. Wynn / Mrs. Smith earlier today
items.original_candidate: (Jeanette) Smith / Mrs. Wynn — possibly two distinct figures
items.new_candidate: Jeanette (Musgrove) Smith — single figure; Whisper's 'Mrs. Wynn' is a phonetic mishearing of 'Jeanette'
items.evidence: The raw Whisper renders the exchange as 'We talked to Mrs. Smith earlier today and she said she was called that white Negro. / Mrs. Wynn, which one? / Jeanette. / Oh, Jeanette, I know it's Jeanette.' The Pass 2 hypothesis posited a separate 'Mrs. Wynn' figure, but archival verification rejects this: Vernon Dahmer Jr.'s CRHP interview was conducted by Emilye Crosby in Hattiesburg on 2015-12-01 (LoC AFC 2010/039 CRHP0114), the SAME day Crosby recorded the joint Raylawni Branch and Jeanette Smith interview (LoC item 2016655403, CRHP0112), making 'Mrs. Smith earlier today' a direct same-day-interview reference. Jeanette Smith's maiden name was Musgrove (Hattiesburg Cultural Center 2020 Jazz Brunch honoring 'Jeanette Musgrove Smith'), which precisely matches the corroborating line three lines later in the same Dahmer passage: 'she was a Musgrove, I know.' The string 'Mrs. Wynn' appears only once in the entire raw transcript — in the very line where the interviewer is asking 'which Jeanette?' — and is absent everywhere else; the corrected file's normalization to 'Jeanette, which one?' is consistent with the surrounding context (interviewer disambiguating the first name after Vernon Jr. said only 'Mrs. Smith'). No separate Mrs. Wynn exists as an SOHP same-day interviewee or in the Dahmer transcript corpus.
